mnemonics.title: DP Table Fill Order
mnemonics.content: Remember: 'Row by Row, Bottom to Top' - fill table from first item row to last, each row left to right from weight 0 to W

mnemonics.title: Greedy vs DP Decision
mnemonics.content: 'Fractional - Greedy, Integer - DP' - For fractional take highest ratio, for 0/1 must consider all combinations via DP

mnemonics.title: Base Cases
mnemonics.content: 'No items = 0, No capacity = 0' - Both K[0][w] and K[i][0] are always 0

acronyms.expansion: Maximum value using first i items with capacity w
acronyms.usage: DP table notation for storing optimal subproblem solutions
Can get (1-ε) approximation for Knapsack in polynomial time
Better approximation with runtime polynomial in both n and 1/ε

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a grid: rows represent items (0 to n), columns represent capacities (0 to W). Each cell stores max value achievable.
Think of items as 'value per unit weight' - like price per kg when shopping for maximum value within budget
At each cell, choose: either exclude current item (above cell) OR include it (value + cell at remaining capacity) - take maximum

analogies.analogy: Filling a fixed-size backpack while robbing houses - either take whole item or leave it, cannot split a TV in half
Filling a backpack with gold dust, coins, and gems - can take portions of each to maximize value
Buying products with highest price/weight ratio at store - may fill bag with few expensive items but miss better combination of cheaper items that together are more valuable
